context-tax.name: context-tax
context-tax.description: Measures character/token overhead of CLI wrapper layer components

systemPromptChars.description: System prompt length assuming 60k per-file limit (code default after PR #383). Actual delivered chars depend on runtime config: local autogent.json still has maxCharsPerFile=20000 (see rawData note), which would truncate to 57,733 chars.

systemPromptTokensEstimated.description: Estimated token count at 60k limit (÷4 heuristic). At 20k limit (local config), estimate is 14,433 tokens.

toolDefinitionsChars.description: Combined length of all 35 tool definitions serialized as JSON. NOTE: Prior baselines used per-tool char estimates (avg ~275 chars); actual serialized JSON is ~932 chars/tool. The May 31 baseline value of 8,240 was a significant underestimate. This baseline uses actual JSON.stringify() measurement.

toolDefinitionsTokensEstimated.description: Estimated token count of tool definitions (÷4 heuristic)

toolCount.description: Number of registered tool definitions (up from 30 on May 31; added: begin_plan, checkpoint_plan, review_pr, verify_deploy, skill_manage)

avgCharsPerTool.description: Average chars per tool definition (actual measurement; prior baselines used estimates of ~275 chars/tool)

totalOverheadChars.description: Total wrapper overhead at 60k per-file limit (137,733 system prompt + 65,477 tools). At 20k limit: 123,210 chars.

totalOverheadTokensEstimated.description: Total estimated token overhead at 60k limit (34,433 system prompt + 16,369 tools)

bootstrapTruncated.description: Both PLAYBOOK.md (133,761 chars) and CONTEXT.md (74,973 chars) now exceed the 60k per-file limit. The PR #383 fix (20k→60k) is no longer sufficient. bootstrapTruncated returns to 1.

rawData.note: Static analysis baseline captured June 14, 2026. PLAYBOOK.md has grown 150% since May 31 (53,583→133,761 chars), CONTEXT.md grew 59% (47,057→74,973 chars). Both now exceed the 60k maxCharsPerFile limit that PR #383 established. Additionally, the local autogent.json runtime config still has maxCharsPerFile=20000 (not updated since before PR #383), meaning the actual runtime truncation is worse than the 60k-limit scenario documented here. Tool definitions measured via actual JSON.stringify() serialization rather than per-file estimates used in prior baselines; this corrects a systematic undercount (prior estimate: ~275 chars/tool, actual: ~932 chars/tool).
